content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Found 1 actionable issue: proposed-bead idempotency can be bypassed across runs because dedupe checks only one representative amendment_id per summary group.

## Amendments

1. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1446-1453`, `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` treats a summary bucket as already reconciled if it finds a single existing `amendment_id` match (`min()` of the group). That only suppresses duplicates when that exact representative ID was written before, so the same `proposed_bead_summary` can be written repeatedly across retries/runs when IDs differ (e.g., new payload run IDs). This causes duplicate entries in `proposed-beads.ndjson` even though proposal idempotency is intended at the handoff level.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Prevents duplicate follow-up proposals accumulating over retries and keeps triage noise low when the same threshold-backed summary keeps being hit.
